feature: Inventory and dependency graph: Performance Schema / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on Performance Schema cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
implementation_detail: Discover Performance Schema, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.

cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for Performance Schema, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Performance Schema.
acceptance_criteria: Given Performance Schema data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Performance Schema connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Discover redo log, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for redo log.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for redo log.
data_model: redo-log_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for redo log cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for redo log; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for redo log.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for redo log, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for redo log.
acceptance_criteria: Given redo log data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: redo log connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate TLS configuration operational-excellence issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for TLS configuration.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for TLS configuration.
data_model: tls-configuration_operational-excellence_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for TLS configuration operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for TLS configuration; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for TLS configuration.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for TLS configuration recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for TLS configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Given TLS configuration data exists, when the operational-excellence agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: TLS configuration connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Detect restore drills symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect resilience.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for restore drills.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for restore drills.
data_model: restore-drills_resilience_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for restore drills resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for restore drills; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for restore drills.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for restore drills recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for restore drills.
acceptance_criteria: Given restore drills data exists, when the resilience health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: restore drills connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
